notes: Use standard chemical notation. For doped systems write full stoichiometry. Parsed by chemparse/pymatgen to extract elemental fractions as ML features.
Primary prediction target for soft ceramics. Leave blank if not measured. Do NOT use DFT-computed values mixed with experimental without flagging.
Second prediction target. Blank is acceptable for composite rows where Tc is not well-defined. Materials Project DFT values can be used for endmembers.

composite_value: Weight fraction of ceramic in polymer matrix
notes: Key feature for composite ML module. Set exactly 0 for all bulk ceramic rows. Non-zero only for composite rows.
Use lowercase. none for all bulk ceramic rows. pvdf = polyvinylidene fluoride. p_vdf_trfe = P(VDF-TrFE) copolymer.

allowed_values_or_example: none | spherical | rod | cube | nanoblock | fiber | platelet
notes: none for bulk ceramic rows. Morphology strongly influences beta-phase nucleation in PVDF matrix.
Leave blank for bulk ceramics. Use average size from paper. For nanorods use average length.

composite_value: Surface modifier applied to filler before mixing
allowed_values_or_example: none | untreated | silane | plasma | acid | peg | dopamine
notes: none for bulk ceramics. untreated = no modification done. silane = silane coupling agent (e.g. APTES/KH550).

allowed_values_or_example: conventional | hot_press | sps | rtgg | tgg | two_step | electrospinning | solvent_cast | cold_sinter | hot_compression
notes: For ceramics use sintering method. For composites use film-making method. rtgg = reactive templated grain growth. sps = spark plasma sintering.
One of the two new prediction targets for Obj 2. Often reported as HV0.5 or HV1 (load in kg). Leave blank if only Qm is reported. Do NOT mix Mohs and Vickers in same column.
Second new prediction target for Obj 2. Inversely correlated with d33 in most compositions — this trade-off is the core of the Pareto optimization in Obj 4*.
Dimensionless. Indicates electromechanical efficiency. Useful as an additional prediction target or feature. Not required but collect if reported in paper.
Strong predictor of both Qm and d33. Calculated from measured bulk density / theoretical XRD density × 100. Often reported in paper as % or g/cm³.
Critical processing feature. Same composition sintered at different temperatures gives different d33 and Qm — include all variants as separate rows.

allowed_values_or_example: conventional | hot_press | sps | rtgg | tgg | two_step | cold_sinter
notes: Must match fabrication_method for ceramic rows. conventional = standard air sintering. sps = low-temperature high-density route.

soft = PPB-engineered high d33 low Qm (wearables). hard = acceptor-doped high Qm low d33 (motors/actuators). composite = PVDF matrix rows. This column enables the use-case mapping required by your synopsis.

bulk_ceramic_value: Full URL to journal paper or database entry
notes: Always include full clickable URL. For Materials Project entries use the material page URL. For papers prefer DOI link over journal page link.

bulk_ceramic_value: Short human-readable description of the data point source
allowed_values_or_example: Journal Year - key result. e.g. ACS AMI 2024 - textured KNN Qm=582 kp=0.58
notes: Format: Journal Abbrev. Year - one-line summary of key reported values. Helps you quickly identify which paper each row came from without opening the DOI.
